config.remediationconfiguration.ConfigRu.Description: The name of the AWS Config rule.

config.remediationconfiguration.MaximumA.Description: The maximum number of failed attempts for auto-remediation. If you do not select a number, the default is 5.  For example, if you specify MaximumAutomaticAttempts as 5 with RetryAttemptSeconds as 50 seconds, AWS Config will put a RemediationException on your behalf for the failing resource after the 5th failed attempt within 50 seconds.

config.remediationconfiguration.Paramete.Description: An object of the RemediationParameterValue.  The type is a map of strings to RemediationParameterValue.

config.remediationconfiguration.RetryAtt.Description: Maximum time in seconds that AWS Config runs auto-remediation. If you do not select a number, the default is 60 seconds.   For example, if you specify RetryAttemptSeconds as 50 seconds and MaximumAutomaticAttempts as 5, AWS Config will run auto-remediations 5 times within 50 seconds before throwing an exception.

config.remediationconfiguration.TargetId.Description: Target ID is the name of the public document.

config.remediationconfiguration.TargetTy.Description: The type of the target. Target executes remediation. For example, SSM document.

config.remediationconfiguration.TargetVe.Description: Version of the target. For example, version of the SSM document.  If you make backward incompatible changes to the SSM document, you must call PutRemediationConfiguration API again to ensure the remediations can run.
